# DECA — Decade of Autonomous Triage — Solution Documentation

> **How to publish:** This is a single, self-contained page — create a new Confluence page
> and paste this Markdown (*Insert → Markup → Markdown*). The architecture diagram is
> embedded inline as a base64 image, so there is **nothing to attach**. If your Confluence
> instance strips data-URI images on paste, regenerate/re-embed with
> `venv/bin/python docs/generate_architecture_png.py` and upload `architecture_diagram.png`
> as a fallback attachment.

**Status:** POC (Proof of Concept) · **Owner:** SRE / Platform AI · **Last updated:** 2026-07-01

---

## Table of contents

1. [Architectural explanation](#1-architectural-explanation)
2. [Services — functionality & technical working](#2-services--functionality--technical-working)
3. [Problem statement](#3-problem-statement)
4. [Benefits](#4-benefits)
5. [How we achieved this](#5-how-we-achieved-this)
6. [Sources of data](#6-sources-of-data)

---

## Executive summary

**DECA — Decade of Autonomous Triage** is a multi-agent, GenAI system that automates
Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) alert triage. It ingests incidents from **ServiceNow** or
**Email**, retrieves the matching **runbook** using semantic search (RAG), runs the runbook's
**diagnostic SQL** against the production audit database, asks **Google Gemini** to produce a
root-cause analysis, and then critiques it, correlates it with historical incidents, raises a
Jira ticket, and delivers a styled **PDF investigation report** back to the analyst's inbox and
the real-time dashboard — end to end, in under a minute per alert.

---

## 1. Architectural explanation

### 1.1 High-level architecture

The platform is a set of small, independently deployable services communicating over HTTP/JSON.
It splits cleanly into four layers:

| Layer | Responsibility | Components |
|-------|----------------|------------|
| **Ingestion** | Capture new alerts from ticketing & email | Mock ServiceNow (5001), Mock Outlook (5002), Email Listener |
| **Application / Dashboard** | Persist incidents, drive the pipeline, stream UI updates | Backend + Frontend (5000), SQLite `triage.db` |
| **Orchestration** | Coordinate the AI triage pipeline | Root Orchestrator (8080) |
| **Specialist agents** | Knowledge, data, review, strategy, ticketing, notification | Knowledge (8001), Postgres (8003), Critic (8004), Concept (8005), Jira (8006), Incident Logger (8007), Notification (8008) |

![Solution architecture and data flow](data:image/png;base64,)

*Figure 1 — End-to-end architecture. Alerts enter from Mock ServiceNow (poll) or Mock
Outlook via the Email Listener (`/api/ingest-alert`); the Backend persists the incident and
calls the Root Orchestrator (`/process-alert`), which fans out to the specialist agents
(Knowledge/RAG, Postgres, Critic, Concept, Jira, Notification). Knowledge and Postgres
agents read evidence from Cloud SQL; the Notification agent emails the PDF report back to
Outlook, and the Backend streams live updates to the Analyst Dashboard over SSE.*

### 1.2 Component & port map

| Service | Port | Framework | Role |
|---------|------|-----------|------|
| Root Orchestrator | 8080 | FastAPI + Vertex AI Gemini | Pipeline coordinator |
| Knowledge Ingestion | 8001 | FastAPI + Gemini + LangChain + pgvector | Runbook RAG store |
| Postgres Agent | 8003 | FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Cloud SQL Connector | SQL execution proxy |
| Critic Agent | 8004 | FastAPI + Gemini | Analysis quality reviewer |
| Concept Agent | 8005 | FastAPI + Gemini + SQLite | Historical pattern correlation |
| Jira Agent | 8006 | FastAPI + Gemini (mock) | Ticket creation |
| Incident Logger | 8007 | FastAPI | Write-back to ServiceNow |
| Notification Agent | 8008 | FastAPI | HTML/PDF report + email delivery |
| Mock ServiceNow (STIP) | 5001 | Flask | ServiceNow Table API simulator |
| Mock Outlook | 5002 | Flask (Graph-compatible) | Mailbox simulator |
| Backend + Frontend | 5000 | Flask + SQLite + SSE | Dashboard & pipeline driver |
| Email Listener | — | Python worker | Mailbox poller → alert forwarder |

### 1.3 Technology stack

- **AI:** Google **Vertex AI Gemini** (`gemini-2.5-flash`) for reasoning; **`text-embedding-004`** (768-dim) for semantic search.
- **Retrieval:** **PostgreSQL + pgvector** cosine similarity over runbook embeddings (RAG).
- **Agent services:** **FastAPI** + **uvicorn** (async microservices).
- **Ingestion & UI:** **Flask** + **Server-Sent Events (SSE)** for live dashboard updates.
- **Databases:** **Cloud SQL (PostgreSQL)** for audit logs & runbooks; **SQLite** (`triage.db`) for incident/pipeline state.
- **Reporting:** **xhtml2pdf** to convert the HTML investigation report into a PDF attachment.
- **Cloud:** **GCS** (runbook PDFs), **Vertex AI**, **Cloud SQL**, designed for **Cloud Run**.

### 1.4 Design principles

- **Separation of concerns** — each agent owns one narrow capability and can be scaled/deployed independently.
- **Structured AI output** — Gemini responses are constrained to a **Pydantic `Analysis` schema** (JSON mode), so downstream agents receive predictable data.
- **Evidence-grounded reasoning** — the LLM only sees the matched runbook + real SQL results, reducing hallucination.
- **Real-time observability** — every pipeline step is streamed to the UI via SSE.
- **Mock-first** — ServiceNow, Outlook/Graph, and Jira are mocked so the full flow runs locally without external credentials, while keeping the exact real-world API contracts.

---

## 2. Services — functionality & technical working

### 2.1 Root Orchestrator (`:8080`)
**File:** `src/agents/root-orchestrator/agent.py` · **FastAPI + Gemini**

The brain of the pipeline. Entry point `POST /process-alert {alert_name}` runs a 5-step flow:

1. **Alert reception.**
2. **Runbook search** — `POST :8001/search {query, top_k}` (semantic RAG).
3. **Diagnostic SQL** — extracts the **CA_ID** from the alert via regex, fills the runbook's `'<CA_ID>'` placeholders, and calls `POST :8003/execute-query` for each query.
4. **Root-cause analysis** — builds an evidence block (alert + runbook + SQL rows) and calls **Gemini in JSON mode**, validated into the `Analysis` Pydantic model.
5. **Post-analysis actions** — calls the **Critic**, **Concept**, **Jira**, and **Notification** agents.

Key functions: `find_runbook()`, `execute_queries()`, `analyze_with_gemini()`, `_compute_confidence()`, `_build_report_context()` (CA_ID extraction, confidence, escalation). Returns a rich JSON payload (`runbook_matched`, `query_results`, `detailed_analysis_report`, `report_context`, `post_analysis_results`).

### 2.2 Knowledge Ingestion (`:8001`)
**File:** `src/agents/knowledge_ingestion/agent.py` · **FastAPI + Gemini + LangChain + pgvector**

The RAG knowledge base.
- `POST /process-and-ingest-pdfs` — lists runbook PDFs from a **GCS bucket**, parses each with **Gemini function-calling** (`create_runbook_from_pdf` → `alert_name`, `investigation_steps`, `sql_queries`, `verification_steps`, `notes`), generates a **768-dim embedding**, and upserts into the PostgreSQL `runbooks` table.
- `POST /search {query, top_k}` — embeds the query and runs a **pgvector cosine search** (`ORDER BY embedding <=> :vec`) to return the best-matching runbook.
- On startup it also seeds ~100 realistic `xs2a_accounts_db.audit_log` records for the demo.

### 2.3 Postgres Agent (`:8003`)
**File:** `src/agents/postgres-agent/agent.py` · **FastAPI + SQLAlchemy + Cloud SQL Connector**

A thin, safe **SQL execution proxy** to Cloud SQL.
- `POST /execute-query {query}` — runs the (already parameter-filled) query via SQLAlchemy with a timeout and returns `{status, data:[...]}` or `{rows_affected}`.
- Connects through the **Cloud SQL Python Connector** (IAM auth, PSC) using `pg8000`.

### 2.4 Critic Agent (`:8004`)
**File:** `src/agents/critic-agent/agent.py` · **FastAPI + Gemini**

Acts as a **principal SRE reviewer**. `POST /evaluate-analysis` scores the analysis on **clarity, actionability, and evidence** (1–5 each) and returns `overall_assessment` + `critique_notes`. Provides a quality gate / second opinion on the AI's output.

### 2.5 Concept Agent (`:8005`)
**File:** `src/agents/concept-agent/agent.py` · **FastAPI + Gemini + SQLite (read-only)**

Adds **strategic, historical context**. `POST /identify-concepts` extracts keywords from the current analysis, queries the last 30 days of incidents in `triage.db`, and asks Gemini to identify **recurring patterns**, **preventive measures**, and a **strategic urgency score** — turning one-off firefighting into systemic insight.

### 2.6 Jira Agent (`:8006`)
**File:** `src/agents/jira-agent/agent.py` · **FastAPI + Gemini (mock mode)**

`POST /create-ticket` uses Gemini to map the analysis into a well-formed **Jira Story** (summary convention `SRE | Investigate | <alert>`, Jira-markup description, priority mapping, labels from affected components). In **mock mode** it returns a synthetic ticket key/URL; real mode would call the Jira REST API.

### 2.7 Incident Logger (`:8007`)
**File:** `src/agents/incident-logger-agent/agent.py` · **FastAPI**

`POST /update-incident` writes findings back to the source ticket by `PATCH`-ing the Mock ServiceNow incident (`work_notes`, `state=6 Resolved`). Closes the loop with the system of record.

### 2.8 Notification Agent (`:8008`)
**Files:** `src/agents/notification-agent/agent.py`, `report_template.py` · **FastAPI**

`POST /send-notification` renders the styled **HTML investigation report** (summary, runbook, confidence, SQL tables, findings, actions, escalation), optionally converts to **PDF (xhtml2pdf)**, and delivers it to the mailbox.

### 2.9 Mock ServiceNow / STIP (`:5001`)
**File:** `src/stip_generater/server.py` · **Flask**

Simulates the **ServiceNow Table API** (`GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /api/now/table/incident`) with an in-memory store seeded with realistic incidents (HighDatabaseConnections, PSD2 Outbound Latency Alert, etc.) and a portal UI. The backend polls this for `state=1` (new).

### 2.10 Mock Outlook (`:5002`)
**File:** `src/email_listener/mock_outlook/server.py` · **Flask (Graph-compatible)**

Mimics **Microsoft Graph** mail endpoints (`GET .../mailFolders/Inbox/messages?$filter=isRead eq false`, `PATCH .../messages/<id>`), plus UI/report extensions: `POST /api/compose` (create an alert), `POST /api/deliver-report` (receive HTML+PDF report), and `GET /api/messages/<id>/attachments/<idx>` (download the PDF). Reports are stored **already-read** so the listener never re-triages them.

### 2.11 Backend + Frontend (`:5000`)
**Files:** `src/backend/server.py`, `database.py` · **Flask + SQLite + SSE**

The control plane and dashboard.
- **`poll_servicenow()`** every 10s creates incidents and launches `process_incident()` threads.
- **`POST /api/ingest-alert`** does the same for email alerts (mirrors the ServiceNow path).
- **`process_incident()`** calls the orchestrator, updates the 6 pipeline steps, persists results, streams SSE events, and delivers the PDF report.
- Serves the UI, `GET /api/incidents`, `GET /api/incidents/<id>/report.html`, `GET /api/dashboard/stats`, and the SSE stream `GET /api/incidents/stream`.

### 2.12 Email Listener
**File:** `src/email_listener/email_listener.py` · **Python worker**

Polls the shared mailbox (real **Microsoft Graph** or the mock, selected by `EMAIL_LISTENER_MODE`), treats each unread subject as an alert, forwards it (`FORWARD_MODE=backend` → `/api/ingest-alert`, or `orchestrator` → `/process-alert`), and marks the message read. Real-Graph behavior is the default; mock mode needs no credentials.

---

## 3. Problem statement

Modern SRE and platform teams face a persistent operational burden:

- **Alert fatigue & manual triage.** On-call engineers manually read each alert, hunt for the right runbook, run diagnostic queries, and piece together a root cause — repetitive, slow, and error-prone.
- **Knowledge trapped in documents.** Runbooks live as PDFs/wiki pages that are hard to search under pressure; institutional knowledge is inconsistently applied.
- **Slow mean-time-to-resolution (MTTR).** Context-switching between ServiceNow, dashboards, databases, and Jira adds minutes-to-hours per incident.
- **Inconsistent quality & no learning loop.** Analyses vary by engineer; recurring/systemic issues go unnoticed because incidents aren't correlated over time.
- **Fragmented tooling.** Ticketing, email, observability data, and databases are disconnected, forcing humans to be the integration layer.

**Goal:** automatically triage an incoming alert end-to-end — retrieve the correct runbook, gather real diagnostic evidence, produce a trustworthy, quality-checked root-cause analysis, file the ticket, and deliver a shareable report — with a human-in-the-loop confidence signal.

---

## 4. Benefits

- **Faster MTTR.** Full triage in well under a minute vs. manual minutes-to-hours.
- **Consistent, evidence-grounded analysis.** Every incident gets the same rigorous, runbook + real-SQL backed treatment; the Critic agent enforces quality.
- **Automated knowledge retrieval (RAG).** PDF runbooks become instantly searchable; the right procedure is applied every time.
- **Human-in-the-loop trust.** A transparent **0–100 confidence score** and explicit **escalation flags** tell engineers when to trust or double-check the AI.
- **Systemic insight, not just firefighting.** The Concept agent surfaces recurring patterns and preventive measures across historical incidents.
- **Reduced context-switching.** Ticket updates, Jira stories, dashboards, and inbox reports are produced automatically.
- **Deliverable artifacts.** A styled **PDF investigation report** lands in the analyst's inbox and on the dashboard for auditability and sharing.
- **Real-time visibility.** Live pipeline progress via SSE.
- **Extensible & cloud-native.** Independent microservices, mockable integrations, and a Cloud Run-ready design.

---

## 5. How we achieved this

### 5.1 Multi-agent orchestration
A central **Root Orchestrator** coordinates specialist agents over HTTP. This keeps each agent
simple and independently testable, and lets the pipeline evolve (add/replace agents) without a
rewrite.

### 5.2 Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
Runbook PDFs are parsed by **Gemini function-calling** into structured JSON, embedded with
**`text-embedding-004`**, and stored in **PostgreSQL/pgvector**. At runtime, the alert is embedded
and matched by **cosine similarity**, so the LLM always reasons over the *correct* procedure.

### 5.3 Evidence-grounded, structured reasoning
The orchestrator fills the runbook's SQL templates with the extracted **CA_ID**, executes them
against **Cloud SQL**, and feeds the **real rows** plus the runbook to Gemini in **JSON mode**.
Output is validated against a **Pydantic `Analysis` model** (`root_cause_summary`,
`key_findings[severity]`, `recommended_actions`, `escalation_needed`, `confidence_score`, …).

### 5.4 Deterministic confidence scoring
When the model doesn't supply a score, `_compute_confidence()` derives one from evidence quality:

| Condition | Penalty |
|-----------|---------|
| No runbook matched | −30 |
| SQL query errored | −20 |
| 0 rows returned (observability gap) | −25 |
| No queries executed | −15 |
| Escalation needed | −15 |
| No key findings | −10 |

Start at 100, clamp to **[5, 100]**. Model bands: **HIGH 85–100**, **MEDIUM 60–84**, **LOW 0–59**.

### 5.5 Quality gate & strategic correlation
The **Critic** agent independently scores clarity/actionability/evidence; the **Concept** agent
correlates against 30 days of history to spot systemic risk — moving beyond single-incident fixes.

### 5.6 Automated action & reporting
The **Jira** agent files a Story; the **Notification** agent renders an HTML report, converts it to
**PDF (xhtml2pdf)**, and delivers it to the inbox (stored already-read to avoid re-triage loops).

### 5.7 Real-time, mock-first delivery
The **Flask backend** persists state to **SQLite**, drives the pipeline in threads, and streams
progress via **SSE**. ServiceNow, Outlook/Graph, and Jira are **mocked with real API contracts**,
so the whole flow runs locally and swaps to production integrations with config only.

### 5.8 Cloud-native footprint
Vertex AI (Gemini + embeddings), Cloud SQL (audit + runbooks), and GCS (runbook PDFs), packaged as
independent services suitable for **Cloud Run**.

---

## 6. Sources of data

| # | Source | Store / Location | Used by | Purpose |
|---|--------|------------------|---------|---------|
| 1 | **Runbook PDFs** | GCS bucket (`KNOWLEDGE_BUCKET_NAME`), local `knowledge_base_pdfs/` | Knowledge Ingestion | Parsed → structured runbooks (investigation steps + SQL) |
| 2 | **Runbook embeddings** | PostgreSQL `runbooks` table (pgvector, 768-dim) | Knowledge Ingestion `/search` | Semantic runbook matching (RAG) |
| 3 | **Audit logs** | Cloud SQL `xs2a_accounts_db.audit_log` (partitioned) | Postgres Agent | Real diagnostic evidence (event_type, code, severity, timestamps) |
| 4 | **Incident & pipeline state** | SQLite `src/backend/triage.db` | Backend, Concept Agent | Incident history, 6-step pipeline status, orchestrator responses |
| 5 | **Mock ServiceNow incidents** | In-memory (`:5001`) | Backend poller | Simulated ticket source (state=1 new) |
| 6 | **Mock Outlook mailbox** | In-memory (`:5002`, Graph-shaped) | Email Listener, Notification | Email-sourced alerts + report delivery |

### 6.1 Knowledge base runbook schema (extracted JSON)
```json
{
  "alert_name": "PSD2Outbound GCO Alert - Latency",
  "investigation_steps": [{"action": "...", "details": "..."}],
  "sql_queries": [{"purpose": "...", "query": "SELECT ... WHERE credit_application_id = '<CA_ID>' ..."}],
  "verification_steps": ["..."],
  "notes": ["..."]
}
```

### 6.2 Cloud SQL audit table (evidence)
```sql
CREATE TABLE xs2a_accounts_db.audit_log (
  audit_id              varchar(36) NOT NULL,
  credit_application_id varchar(255),   -- e.g. "MMDEOPRA 123-45-00000001 123456-789012 12345678-0"
  event_type            varchar(50),    -- CONSENT_CREATION, TRANSACTION_FETCH, ...
  code                  numeric,        -- HTTP status: 200, 401, 403, 500, 503
  description           varchar(1024),
  severity              varchar(20),    -- INFO, WARN, ERROR, CRITICAL
  created_date_time     timestamp NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
) PARTITION BY RANGE (created_date_time);
```

### 6.3 Incident tracking (`triage.db`)
```sql
CREATE TABLE incidents (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  snow_sys_id TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, number TEXT NOT NULL,
  short_description TEXT, description TEXT,
  priority INTEGER, priority_label TEXT, category TEXT, assignment_group TEXT,
  opened_at TIMESTAMP, created_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, completed_at TIMESTAMP,
  state TEXT NOT NULL,             -- in_progress | completed | failed
  orchestrator_response TEXT,      -- full orchestrator JSON
  ai_decision TEXT                 -- "Report Generated" or error
);

CREATE TABLE pipeline_steps (
  id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  incident_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
  step_number INTEGER NOT NULL,    -- 1..6
  step_name TEXT NOT NULL,         -- Alert Reception, Runbook Search, ...
  status TEXT NOT NULL,            -- pending | running | completed | failed
  details TEXT,
  FOREIGN KEY (incident_id) REFERENCES incidents (id)
);
```

---

## Appendix A — Inter-service API contract

| From | To | Method | Endpoint | Payload |
|------|----|--------|----------|---------|
| Backend | Mock ServiceNow | GET | `/api/now/table/incident` | `sysparm_query=state=1` |
| Email Listener | Backend | POST | `/api/ingest-alert` | `{alert_name, sender, source}` |
| Backend | Orchestrator | POST | `/process-alert` | `{alert_name}` |
| Orchestrator | Knowledge | POST | `/search` | `{query, top_k}` |
| Orchestrator | Postgres | POST | `/execute-query` | `{query}` |
| Orchestrator | Critic | POST | `/evaluate-analysis` | `{analysis}` |
| Orchestrator | Concept | POST | `/identify-concepts` | `{analysis}` |
| Orchestrator | Jira | POST | `/create-ticket` | `{summary, description, incident_id}` |
| Orchestrator | Notification | POST | `/send-notification` | `{incident_id, summary, ...}` |
| Notification / Backend | Mock Outlook | POST | `/api/deliver-report` | `{subject, body_html, attachments[]}` |
| Email Listener | Mock Outlook | GET | `/v1.0/users/{mailbox}/mailFolders/Inbox/messages` | `$filter=isRead eq false` |

## Appendix B — The 6 pipeline steps

| # | Step | Produced by |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | Alert Reception | Backend |
| 2 | Parameter Extraction | Orchestrator (CA_ID) |
| 3 | Runbook Search | Knowledge Ingestion (RAG) |
| 4 | Diagnostic Queries | Postgres Agent |
| 5 | AI Root Cause Analysis | Gemini (Orchestrator) |
| 6 | Post-Analysis Actions | Critic + Concept + Jira + Notification |
